title: Adjacent Possible

content.statement: Innovation grows from the immediate vicinity of current capabilities.
content.question: What is the adjacent possible move that uses what I already have to open something new?
content.equation: Innovation Yield = (Adjacent Steps + Combination Quality + Iteration Speed) ÷ 3
content.mental_model: Door to the next room, not the whole house at once.
content.business_lens: Build the next product from current capabilities and adjacent customer needs; avoid leaping to far-future markets without intermediate steps.
content.life_lens: Grow by stretching one step beyond current capacity, not by demanding a full transformation.
sop: Identify customer needs one step beyond what you serve
Repeat, each step opens the next
content.explainer: Instagram emerged adjacent to existing photo apps by adding filters and a social feed. The adjacent possible explains why category-creating innovations usually look obvious in hindsight: they were one step away the whole time, but only visible from the vicinity.
questions: What is the adjacent possible move that uses what I already have to open something new?
List five things your business could become next, using only what you already have. Which one have you dismissed because it feels too obvious?
What underused asset, data, relationships, a skill you built by accident, is quietly capable of opening a door you haven't tried?

definitions.def: The set of next moves that become available only because of what you've already built or learned, not moves available from scratch.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Adjacent Steps 7 (how many nearby moves you can see from here), Combination Quality 6 (how well your existing pieces fit together), Iteration Speed 8 (how fast you can test the next step). Score = (7 + 6 + 8) ÷ 3 = 7.0. Higher means you're compounding capability into new opportunity efficiently; lower means you're either not seeing the adjacent moves or too slow to test them.
content.closing_statement: The next move is usually closer than it looks.

caption:
Principle Nº 306: Adjacent Possible

Innovation grows from the immediate vicinity of current capabilities. Instagram emerged adjacent to existing photo apps by adding filters and a social feed.

Ask yourself: What is the adjacent possible move that uses what I already have to open something new?
